commonProblems.h2: Vauxhall Grandland X Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense

problems.group: Vauxhall Grandland X 1.2 PureTech Wet Timing Belt Failure
problems.h4: Vauxhall Grandland X 1.2 PureTech Wet Timing Belt Failure - Why EB2 Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Grandland X SE, SRi, Elite, Ultimate (2017-2024, EB2ADTS, EB2ADT, EB2DTS, EB2F)
problems.rootCause: The wet timing belt runs in oil; degradation causes rubber fragments to block the oil pickup, starving the engine of lubrication and leading to seizure.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove sump, replace wet belt kit, flush oil system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught before belt fragments; prevents catastrophic failure

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace belt, oil pump, clean galleries; may require partial strip
repairOptions.longevity: Only suitable if bottom end undamaged; high risk of hidden damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded belt system, bench-tested, warranty included
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended once oil starvation suspected; eliminates recurrence risk

problems.recommendation: If your Grandland X 1.2 PureTech shows belt noise, oil pressure warnings, or metallic debris in the sump, replacement is almost always more economical than repair. On a vehicle worth £8,000-£15,000, a rebuilt EB2 engine with upgraded belt components offers the safest long-term solution.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2ADTS engine prices for your Vauxhall Grandland X

problems.group: Vauxhall Grandland X DPF & AdBlue System Failure
problems.h4: Vauxhall Grandland X DPF & AdBlue System Failure - Why Diesel Owners Face Costly Repairs
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Grandland X 1.5/1.6 CDTi/BlueHDi (2017-2024, DV5RC, B16DTH, D15DTH)
problems.rootCause: Short-journey driving prevents DPF regeneration; AdBlue injector/nozzle faults trigger limp mode; combined with EGR carbon buildup, repair costs escalate quickly.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Off-car DPF clean, AdBlue system diagnostic reset, EGR clean
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage faults; may recur if driving pattern unchanged

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace DPF, AdBlue injector, NOx sensor; EGR valve service
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine mechanically sound; labour-intensive on PSA platform

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt DV5RC/B16DTH unit with updated emissions hardware
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo/injectors also worn; eliminates multiple failure points

problems.recommendation: If your Grandland X diesel has persistent DPF warnings, AdBlue faults, AND turbo/injector issues, a rebuilt engine often represents better value than stacking individual repairs. Especially on higher-mileage examples, replacement future-proofs your investment.
problems.cta: Get quotes for DV5RC Vauxhall Grandland X diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Vauxhall Grandland X Turbocharger Failure - Oil Starvation & Coking Issues
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Grandland X 1.2 PureTech, 1.6 THP, 1.5/1.6 Diesel (2017-2024, multiple codes)
problems.rootCause: Turbo oil feed coking from infrequent servicing; wet belt debris (petrol) or DPF soot (diesel) contaminating oil; VNT actuator sticking on diesel variants.

repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses boost control faults; not mechanical turbo damage

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit new/remanufactured turbo; oil/feed pipe replacement
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if engine otherwise healthy; risk of repeat if root cause not addressed

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine with Turbo
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete rebuilt unit including upgraded turbo, full oil system flush
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if turbo failure accompanied by oil starvation signs or high mileage

problems.recommendation: Turbo failure on a Grandland X is rarely isolated. If oil pressure was low, metallic debris is present, or the vehicle has exceeded 90k miles, a rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo and oil system components delivers significantly better long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare turbo-inclusive rebuilt engine prices for your Vauxhall Grandland X

problems.group: Vauxhall Grandland X Hybrid Cooling System Fault
problems.h4: Vauxhall Grandland X Hybrid Cooling System Fault - PHEV-Specific Risks
problems.affectedModels: Vauxhall Grandland X Hybrid 225, Hybrid4 300 (2020-2024, EP6FADTXD)
problems.rootCause: Complex dual cooling loops for engine/battery; pump failures or leaks cause overheating; high-voltage system interlocks prevent operation.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace electric coolant pump, thermostat, bleed system
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for isolated cooling faults; requires hybrid-certified technician

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Address overheating damage, replace head gasket, update cooling hardware
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if hybrid battery/e-motors unaffected; complex labour on PSA PHEV platform

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Hybrid Power Unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt EP6FADTXD unit with updated cooling, hybrid system diagnostic reset
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for severe overheating or combined engine/electrical faults

problems.recommendation: Hybrid Grandland X cooling faults require specialist diagnosis. If the combustion engine shows overheating damage, replacement with a rebuilt unit that includes updated cooling components is the most reliable path-especially given the high cost of hybrid-specific repairs.
problems.cta: Enquire about Hybrid EP6FADTXD engine availability for your Vauxhall Grandland X

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Vauxhall Grandland X
finalCta.paragraph: The Grandland X's modern PSA engines demand specialist knowledge for reliable repair or replacement. EngineMarket connects you with UK specialists who understand wet belt risks, AdBlue complexities, and hybrid system integration. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, bench testing, and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty-with nationwide supply & fit available. Compare prices from vetted specialists today and keep your Grandland X on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Vauxhall Grandland X Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Vauxhall Grandland X and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.description: The 1.5L BlueHDi replaced the earlier 1.6 CDTi from 2018 onward, becoming the dominant diesel in the Grandland X range. It offers improved fuel economy but introduced AdBlue complexity and timing chain concerns that drive replacement demand.
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall Grandland X 1.5L diesel engines

items.title: Vauxhall Grandland X 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The original 1.6L CDTi powered early Grandland X models (2017-2018) before the 1.5L update. Robust mechanically but prone to DPF issues on short-journey driving, making it a frequent replacement candidate.
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall Grandland X 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Vauxhall Grandland X 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The high-output 2.0L CDTi biturbo was offered on top Elite/Ultimate trims for strong performance. Complex twin-turbo hardware and higher repair costs make replacement a common consideration for high-mileage examples.
items.cta: Get quotes for Vauxhall Grandland X 2.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Vauxhall Grandland X Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Vauxhall Grandland X 1.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.2L PureTech is the most common Grandland X engine-and the most notorious for wet timing belt degradation. Replacement demand is exceptionally high due to catastrophic failure risk if belt fragments block the oil pickup.
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall Grandland X 1.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Vauxhall Grandland X 1.6L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.6L THP petrol offered stronger performance on pre-facelift Elite/Ultimate trims. Shared with Peugeot, it faces HPFP and turbo reliability concerns that drive replacement searches among performance-oriented owners.
items.cta: Compare Vauxhall Grandland X 1.6L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Vauxhall Grandland X Hybrid / Electrified Engine Sizes

items.title: Vauxhall Grandland X Hybrid Engines
items.description: The Grandland X Hybrid and Hybrid4 use a 1.6L THP petrol engine (EP6FADTXD) combined with electric motor(s) for 225hp or 300hp system output. Replacement requires hybrid-specialist knowledge due to cooling system and high-voltage integration. Not sure which engine size your Vauxhall Grandland X needs? items.title: Vauxhall Grandland X Diesel Engines

D20DTH - rare 2.0L biturbo (2017-2019), high-performance but complex

engineYears.h2: Vauxhall Grandland X Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Grandland X production timeline (2017-2024), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Grandland X launched with PSA's 1.2L PureTech petrol and 1.6L CDTi diesel, sharing underpinnings with the Peugeot 3008.
keyChanges: Grandland X replaces Antara in Vauxhall SUV lineup
PSA EMP2 platform debut for Vauxhall brand
Initial engine lineup: EB2ADT petrol, B16DTH diesel
Trim levels: SE, SRi, Elite, Business Edition
knownFor: Strong initial sales driven by SUV demand and competitive pricing
Early reports of 1.2L PureTech oil consumption concerns
1.6L diesel robust but DPF issues on urban use
High residual values supporting replacement demand
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017 Vauxhall Grandland X replacement engines
years.ticker: Grandland X 1.2 oil consumption • B16DTH DPF warning • EB2ADT timing belt inspection

years.description: Key expansion year: 1.5L BlueHDi replaced 1.6 CDTi for improved efficiency; 1.6L THP petrol added for performance trims.
keyChanges: DV5RC 1.5L BlueHDi replaces B16DTH 1.6L diesel
EP6FDT 1.6L THP petrol introduced on Elite/Ultimate
AdBlue system standard on all diesel variants
Ultimate trim launched with enhanced equipment
knownFor: DV5RC becomes the dominant diesel engine for Grandland X
First widespread reports of EB2ADTS wet belt concerns
1.6L THP praised for performance but HPFP reliability questions
High fleet adoption driving used market supply

years.ticker: DV5RC AdBlue fault • EB2ADTS belt noise • EP6FDT turbo rattle

years.description: Mid-cycle updates focused on emissions compliance and hybrid system development ahead of 2020 PHEV launch.
keyChanges: WLTP emissions testing impacts engine calibration
Minor interior updates and infotainment improvements
Hybrid powertrain testing in pre-production units
Business Edition trim expanded for fleet buyers
mainEngines: EP6FDT - 1.6L petrol (final year before hybrid adaptation)
popularModels: Vauxhall Grandland X 1.2 PureTech Business Edition
knownFor: Growing forum discussion around PureTech wet belt longevity
Diesel variants face increasing DPF/AdBlue repair costs
Strong used values supporting replacement over scrappage decisions
Early hybrid prototypes inform production design
years.cta: Get quotes for 2019-2020 Vauxhall Grandland X replacement engines

years.description: PHEV variants debut with 225hp Hybrid and 300hp Hybrid4 AWD, using adapted EP6FADTXD engine with electric motor integration.
keyChanges: Grandland X Hybrid 225 and Hybrid4 300 launched
EP6FADTXD engine adapted for PHEV cooling/electrical systems
Plug-in charging capability; 25-35 mile electric range
Ultimate trim standard on hybrid variants
knownFor: Hybrid variants command premium pricing and specialist replacement demand
First reports of PHEV cooling system faults affecting engine operation
Non-hybrid models continue high PureTech replacement volume
Strong company car adoption due to PHEV tax benefits

years.preview: Facelift Launch: Updated Styling & Engine Revisions
years.description: Facelift introduces revised styling, updated infotainment, and engine code revisions (EB2DTS, D15DTH) while retaining core architectures.
keyChanges: Exterior refresh: new grille, LED lighting, bumper redesign
Interior: updated infotainment, digital instrument cluster option
Engine codes updated: EB2DTS (1.2L), D15DTH (1.5L), D16XHT (1.6L)
Hybrid variants continue with EP6FADTXD
knownFor: Facelift models retain wet belt risk on 1.2L despite code change
D15DTH diesel shows improved but not eliminated timing chain concerns
Hybrid variants remain specialist-replacement territory
Strong used demand supporting replacement investment decisions

years.description: Final production phase maintains facelift engine lineup; focus shifts to used market replacement demand as new Grandland X production winds down.

Grandland X production ends in 2024 ahead of next-generation model
Used market replacement demand grows as vehicles age
Hybrid variants remain in demand for company car sector

Vauxhall Grandland X 1.5 BlueHDi Business Edition
knownFor: High-mileage PureTech examples drive replacement searches

faq.h2: Vauxhall Grandland X Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Vauxhall Grandland X?
items.answer: Yes, if your Grandland X is otherwise in good condition and under £12,000 value. A rebuilt engine with warranty often costs less than dealer repair and extends vehicle life significantly.
keyPoints: Rebuilt PureTech engines address wet belt failure risk
Diesel replacements resolve DPF/AdBlue complexity economically
Hybrid specialist rebuilds preserve high residual values
12-month warranty provides peace of mind vs used units
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Vauxhall Grandland X engine prices for your variant

items.question: How much does a Vauxhall Grandland X engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: Typical UK rebuilt engine prices range from £1,400 (1.2 PureTech) to £7,500 (Hybrid4), supply-only. Supply & fit adds £600-£1,400 depending on variant complexity.
keyPoints: 1.2L PureTech rebuilt: £1,400-£3,800 supply-only
1.5L Diesel rebuilt: £1,600-£4,300 supply-only
Hybrid4 rebuilt: £3,500-£8,200 supply-only
Prices vary by engine code, supplier location and warranty terms
items.cta: Get a tailored Vauxhall Grandland X engine quote today

items.question: What are the symptoms of a failed Grandland X PureTech timing belt?
items.answer: Warning signs include rattling on cold start, oil pressure warning light, metallic debris in sump, or sudden loss of power. Immediate inspection is critical to prevent seizure.
keyPoints: Belt fragments block oil pickup causing starvation
Metallic knocking or rattling from timing cover area
Oil pressure warning or EML with P0521/P0522 codes
Catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement
items.cta: Compare rebuilt EB2ADTS engine prices for your Vauxhall Grandland X

items.question: How long does a Vauxhall Grandland X engine last?
items.answer: With proper servicing, Grandland X engines can exceed 150,000 miles. However, the 1.2L PureTech wet belt requires proactive replacement around 60k miles to avoid premature failure.
keyPoints: Diesel DV5RC units often reach 120k+ miles with maintenance
PureTech longevity depends on wet belt inspection/replacement
Hybrid variants require cooling system attention for longevity
Full service history significantly extends engine life expectancy
items.cta: Find a reconditioned Vauxhall Grandland X engine with warranty

items.question: Do you supply and fit Vauxhall Grandland X engines nationwide?
items.answer: Yes, our UK network of vetted specialists offers supply-only or full supply & fit for all Grandland X variants, including Hybrid4 and facelift models requiring PSA-certified technicians.
keyPoints: Nationwide coverage with local fitting partners
Hybrid variants require specialist PSA-certified workshops
Supply & fit packages include ECU coding and road test
Next-day engine delivery available for urgent replacements
items.cta: Find Vauxhall Grandland X engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: What warranty comes with a Vauxhall Grandland X replacement engine?
items.answer: All engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Terms vary by supplier-always confirm coverage details before purchase.
keyPoints: Minimum 12-month warranty standard across network
Unlimited mileage coverage on most rebuilt/remanufactured units
Warranty covers parts and labour if supply & fit booked
Hybrid specialist warranties may have specific terms
items.cta: Compare warranty terms when getting Vauxhall Grandland X quotes

items.question: Can I get a Vauxhall Grandland X hybrid engine replaced?
items.answer: Yes, specialist rebuilders in our network handle Grandland X Hybrid and Hybrid4 replacements, including EP6FADTXD engine units with updated cooling and hybrid system calibration.
keyPoints: Hybrid replacement requires PSA-certified technician
Cooling system inspection essential during engine swap
High-voltage safety procedures followed by all specialists
Lead times may be longer than standard engine replacements
items.cta: Enquire about Hybrid EP6FADTXD engine availability for your Grandland X

items.question: What is the difference between reconditioned and used Grandland X engines?
items.answer: Reconditioned engines are stripped, cleaned and rebuilt with new wear components plus warranty. Used engines are low-mileage donor units with variable history and shorter coverage.
keyPoints: Reconditioned: new bearings, seals, timing components included
Used: verify mileage, service history and compression test results
Reconditioned offers better long-term reliability for PureTech
Used may suit older/lower-value Grandland X examples
items.cta: Compare reconditioned vs used Vauxhall Grandland X engine options

items.question: How do I find the engine code on my Vauxhall Grandland X?
items.answer: The engine code is stamped on the engine block near the timing cover, listed on the V5C registration document, or visible on the engine bay VIN plate. EB2ADTS, DV5RC and EP6FADTXD are common codes.
keyPoints: Check V5C Section D.2 for engine code reference
Physical stamp location: timing cover area or cylinder head
DVLA vehicle enquiry service confirms code by registration
Correct code essential for compatible replacement ordering
items.cta: Get quotes using your exact Vauxhall Grandland X engine code

items.question: Can a Peugeot 3008 engine fit a Vauxhall Grandland X?
items.answer: Yes, many PSA-platform engines are compatible between Grandland X and Peugeot 3008 due to shared architecture. However, ECU coding, emissions hardware and trim-specific components require verification.
keyPoints: EB2, DV5, EP6 engine families shared across PSA platform
ECU software and immobiliser may need adaptation
Emissions hardware (AdBlue, DPF) must match variant spec
Always confirm compatibility by engine code, not just model
items.cta: Compare compatible PSA-platform engines for your Vauxhall Grandland X
